Package org.neuroml1.model.morph

Class Morphology

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.neuroml1.model.morph.Morphology
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Cloneable, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.CopyTo2, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.Equals2, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.HashCode2, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.ToString2
    public class Morphology
extends Object
implements Cloneable, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.CopyTo2, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.Equals2, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.HashCode2, org.jvnet.jaxb2_commons.lang.ToString2
    The main element which details the neuronal morphology. Cells, various histological features, and properties associated with the data can be contained in this element

    Java class for Morphology complex type.

    The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class. 

     <complexType name="Morphology">
   <complexContent>
     <restriction base="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}anyType">
       <sequence>
         <group ref="{http://morphml.org/metadata/schema}metadata"/>
         <element name="cells" type="{http://morphml.org/morphml/schema}Cells"/>
         <element name="features" minOccurs="0">
           <complexType>
             <complexContent>
               <restriction base="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}anyType">
                 <sequence>
                   <group ref="{http://morphml.org/metadata/schema}metadata"/>
                   <element name="feature" type="{http://morphml.org/morphml/schema}Feature" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
                 </sequence>
               </restriction>
             </complexContent>
           </complexType>
         </element>
         <element name="propertyDetails" minOccurs="0">
           <complexType>
             <complexContent>
               <restriction base="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}anyType">
                 <sequence>
                   <element name="propertyDetail" type="{http://morphml.org/metadata/schema}PropertyDetail" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
                 </sequence>
               </restriction>
             </complexContent>
           </complexType>
         </element>
         <element name="groupDetails" minOccurs="0">
           <complexType>
             <complexContent>
               <restriction base="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}anyType">
                 <sequence>
                   <element name="groupDetail" type="{http://morphml.org/metadata/schema}GroupDetail" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
                 </sequence>
               </restriction>
             </complexContent>
           </complexType>
         </element>
       </sequence>
       <attribute name="name" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" />
       <attribute name="lengthUnits" type="{http://morphml.org/metadata/schema}LengthUnits" />
       <attribute name="length_units" type="{http://morphml.org/metadata/schema}LengthUnits" />
       <attribute name="volumeUnits" type="{http://morphml.org/metadata/schema}VolumeUnits" default="cubic_millimeter" />
     </restriction>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

      • getGroup

        public List<String> getGroup()
        Gets the value of the group property.

        This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the group property.

        For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

            getGroup().add(newItem);

        Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list String
